Operation 'Match' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description is comprehensive but lacks clarity on what the operation returns and the specific side effects of each match action. Added explicit information about return value and clarified that this is a single SKU operation (vs bulk). Removed permission details from description as they are typically handled separately in API documentation.

data.current_description:
All SKUs sent from a seller to a marketplace must be reviewed and matched. Actions in the matching process are added in the request body through the [matchType] object. Match type actions include: 

1. `newproduct`: Match the SKU as a new product. 

2. `itemMatch`: Associate the received SKU to an existing SKU. 

3. `productMatch`: Associate the received SKU to an existing product. 

4. `deny`: Deny the received SKU. 

5. `pending`: The received SKU requires attention. 

6. `incomplete`: The received SKU is lacking information to be matched. 

7. `insufficientScore`: The Score given by the Matcher to this received SKU doesn't qualify it to be matched. 

Note that  if the autoApprove setting is enabled, the SKUs will be approved, regardless of the Score.

## Permissions

Any user or [application key](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/authentication-overview#application-keys) must have at least one of the appropriate [License Manager resources](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/license-manager-resources--3q6ztrC8YynQf6rdc6euk3) to be able to successfully run this request. Otherwise, they will receive a status code `403` error. These are the applicable resources for this endpoint: 

| **Product** | **Category** | **Resource** |
| ---------- | ------------ | ------------ |
| Channels | UI resources | **Save suggestion rules** |
| Suggestion | Suggestion resources | **Main** |


There are no applicable [predefined roles](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/predefined-roles--jGDurZKJHvHJS13LnO7Dy) for this resource list. You must [create a custom role](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/roles--7HKK5Uau2H6wxE1rH5oRbc#creating-a-role) and add at least one of the resources above in order to use this endpoint. 

To learn more about machine authentication at VTEX, see [Authentication overview](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/authentication-overview#machine-authentication). 

>❗ To prevent integrations from having excessive permissions, consider the [best practices for managing app keys](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/best-practices-application-keys--7b6nD1VMHa49aI5brlOvJm) when assigning License Manager roles to integrations.
data.description_suggestion: Processes a single SKU suggestion match action for a specific seller SKU version. The operation accepts a matchType action in the request body to determine how the received SKU should be handled: `newproduct` (create as new product), `itemMatch` (associate to existing SKU), `productMatch` (associate to existing product), `deny` (reject), `pending` (flag for review), `incomplete` (mark as lacking information), or `insufficientScore` (matcher score insufficient). Returns the updated match result. If autoApprove is enabled, SKUs are automatically approved regardless of matcher score. Requires appropriate License Manager permissions.
data.reasoning: The current description is comprehensive but lacks clarity on what the operation returns and the specific side effects of each match action. Added explicit information about return value and clarified that this is a single SKU operation (vs bulk). Removed permission details from description as they are typically handled separately in API documentation.

message: Operation 'MatchMultiple' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description lacks clarity on the return value (array of results) and doesn't explicitly state that all SKUs in the batch receive the same action. Added information about the response format and clarified the batch processing behavior. Removed permission details as they are typically handled separately.

data.current_description:
Allows a marketplace to bulk approve, deny, or associate up to 25 received SKUs from sellers.

Through the `actionName` attribute, you can select the operation you want to apply to the received SKU. Actions include: 

* `newproduct`: Match the SKU as a new product. 

* `skuassociation`: Associate the received SKU to an existing SKU. 

* `productassociation`: Associate the received SKU to an existing product. 

* `deny`: Deny the received SKU.

## Permissions

Any user or [application key](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/authentication-overview#application-keys) must have at least one of the appropriate [License Manager resources](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/license-manager-resources--3q6ztrC8YynQf6rdc6euk3) to be able to successfully run this request. Otherwise, they will receive a status code `403` error. These are the applicable resources for this endpoint: 

| **Product** | **Category** | **Resource** |
| ---------- | ------------ | ------------ |
| Channels | UI resources | **Save suggestion rules** |
| Suggestion | Suggestion resources | **Main** |


There are no applicable [predefined roles](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/predefined-roles--jGDurZKJHvHJS13LnO7Dy) for this resource list. You must [create a custom role](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/roles--7HKK5Uau2H6wxE1rH5oRbc#creating-a-role) and add at least one of the resources above in order to use this endpoint. 

To learn more about machine authentication at VTEX, see [Authentication overview](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/authentication-overview#machine-authentication). 

>❗ To prevent integrations from having excessive permissions, consider the [best practices for managing app keys](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/best-practices-application-keys--7b6nD1VMHa49aI5brlOvJm) when assigning License Manager roles to integrations.
data.description_suggestion: Bulk processes match actions for up to 25 received SKU suggestions from sellers in a single request. The `actionName` parameter specifies the operation to apply to all SKUs in the batch: `newproduct` (create as new products), `skuassociation` (associate to existing SKUs), `productassociation` (associate to existing products), or `deny` (reject). Returns an array containing the match result for each processed SKU. All SKUs in the batch receive the same action. Requires appropriate License Manager permissions.
data.reasoning: The current description lacks clarity on the return value (array of results) and doesn't explicitly state that all SKUs in the batch receive the same action. Added information about the response format and clarified the batch processing behavior. Removed permission details as they are typically handled separately.

data.current_summary: Activate autoApprove setting for a seller
data.current_description: This endpoint enables the auto approve setting to received SKUs from a specific seller. Be aware that once enabling the rule through this request, all received SKUs from that seller will be automatically approved on your store, regardless of the Matcher Score.
data.description_suggestion: Enables automatic approval for all SKU suggestions received from a specific seller. Once activated, all SKUs from this seller will be automatically approved regardless of Matcher Score. Returns the updated auto-approval configuration. This action affects all future SKU submissions from the specified seller and cannot be selectively applied to individual SKUs.

data.current_description:
This request is used by the seller when it wants to suggest that one of their SKUs is sold in the marketplace.

Before using this request, the seller should always use the [Change Notification](https://developers.vtex.com/vtex-rest-api/reference/catalog-api-get-seller-sku-notification) request in order to check if the SKU already exists in the marketplace. If it doesn't, then this is the next call in the SKU integration flow.

In the Send Suggestion request, the seller must send information about the SKU, such as the product and SKU name, the seller ID, and the image URL. All parameters are explained below.
data.description_suggestion: Submits a new SKU suggestion from a seller to the marketplace for approval. The seller should first verify the SKU doesn't already exist using Change Notification. This request requires SKU details including product name, SKU name, seller ID, and image URL. Returns the created suggestion with its initial Matcher score and approval status. The suggestion will be evaluated against marketplace rules and may be automatically approved if autoApprove is enabled.
data.reasoning: The current description explains the purpose and prerequisites well, but doesn't specify what the endpoint returns or how the suggestion will be processed. Added information about the response structure, Matcher evaluation, and autoApprove behavior to improve AI agent understanding of the operation's effects.

data.current_description: This endpoint deletes a chosen SKU suggestion. Only one SKU should be deleted per request. This action cannot be undone. A workaround to revert a deletion is to send the suggestion again, through the Send Suggestion API.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a specific SKU suggestion from the marketplace. Only one SKU suggestion can be deleted per request. This action is irreversible and cannot be undone. Returns a success confirmation. To restore a deleted suggestion, resubmit it using the Send SKU Suggestion endpoint.

message: Operation 'GetVersions' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary. The summary 'Get all versions' is too generic and lacks domain context. Changed to 'Retrieve all versions of a SKU suggestion' to be more specific and actionable. The description is comprehensive and clearly explains version tracking, the purpose of retrieving versions, and the response structure including the latestversion object.

data.current_description:
Whenever a SKU suggestion is updated or changed, a new version of the original one is created. All versions are logged, so you can search for previous our current states of SKU suggestions. 

This endpoint retrieves the data of *all* previous and latest versions of a specific SKU suggestion, sent by the seller. Whenever a SKU is updated, it is important to map previous versions, to compare and identify changes. 

The response's object [latestversion] provides the information of the most recent version of that SKU suggestion.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve all versions of a SKU suggestion
data.reasoning: The summary 'Get all versions' is too generic and lacks domain context. Changed to 'Retrieve all versions of a SKU suggestion' to be more specific and actionable. The description is comprehensive and clearly explains version tracking, the purpose of retrieving versions, and the response structure including the latestversion object.

data.current_summary: Get autoApprove status in account settings
data.current_description:
This endpoint can be used to check whether the autoapprove setting is active or not, for a specific seller. 

If the response is `true`, the autoapprove setting is active. If the response is `false`, it is inactive.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current autoApprove setting status for the marketplace account. Returns a boolean value: `true` if autoApprove is enabled (all received SKUs are automatically approved), `false` if disabled (SKUs require manual or matcher-based approval). This setting applies account-wide to all sellers unless overridden by seller-specific configurations.
data.reasoning: Original summary is vague about scope (account vs seller). Description mentions 'specific seller' but endpoint path suggests account-level setting. Added clarity about what the boolean response means in operational terms and how it relates to SKU approval workflow. Improved AI agent understanding of when to use this endpoint.

data.current_description:
This endpoint enables the autoapprove rule to a marketplace's whole Received SKUs module. Once enabling the rule, received SKUs will be automatically approved on your store, regardless of the seller. 

 For the autoapprove rule to work as expected, the approval [Matcher score](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/entendendo-a-pontuacao-do-vtex-matcher--tutorials_424) should be set up as 80 (default value), but you can configure a different number through the field `Score` in [Save Account's Approval Settings](https://developers.vtex.com/vtex-rest-api/reference/saveaccountconfig).
data.description_suggestion: Enables or disables automatic approval of received SKUs at the account level. When enabled, all SKUs received from sellers are automatically approved regardless of Matcher score. When disabled, SKUs require manual approval or Matcher-based evaluation. The request body specifies the desired state. Returns the updated autoApprove configuration. Note: Matcher score (default 80) can be customized via Save Account's Approval Settings endpoint. This setting applies account-wide unless overridden by seller-specific configurations.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity on what the request body contains and what is returned. Missing information about side effects (account-wide impact, override behavior). Added details about request/response, operational impact, and relationship to other settings for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description:
Marketplaces use this endpoint to create or update approval settings on their Received SKUs module. 

The request includes all the details necessary to implement the chosen approval settings.
data.description_suggestion: Creates or updates the marketplace account's SKU approval settings for the Received SKUs module. The request body specifies Matcher scores, Matcher configurations, specification mappings, and MatchFlux approval workflow (default, manual, or autoApprove). Returns the updated approval configuration including all configured matchers, score thresholds, and specification mappings. Changes apply account-wide and affect how all received SKUs are evaluated and approved.
data.reasoning: Description is too vague about what 'all the details' means. Missing specifics about request body structure (Score, Matchers, SpecificationsMapping, MatchFlux) and response content. No clarity on scope of changes or side effects. Added concrete details about configurable fields and operational impact for AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'Getmatchconfig' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, unclear_purpose. Original description is unclear about what 'matcher's current approval settings' means and conflates matcher configuration with approval settings. Relationship to Get Account Approval Settings is confusing. Added clarity about Matcher's purpose (comparing SKU data), what is returned, and when to use this vs. the related endpoint. Improved AI agent understanding of the distinction between matcher configuration and broader approval settings.

data.current_description:
This endpoint will be used by the marketplace to query the matcher's current approval settings on their incoming SKUs module. If the account has no matcher configured, the response will be `404 Not Found`. 

For account approval configuration information, with response including specification information, see [Get Account Approval Settings](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/api-reference/marketplace-apis-suggestions#get-/suggestions/configuration) terminal. 

The [VTEX matcher](https://help.vtex.com/pt/tutorial/entendendo-a-punctuacao-do-vtex-matcher--tutorials_424) is a tool used by marketplaces to evaluate SKUs corresponding to information from an advertisement of the seller, with those of items already present in the marketplace catalog.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve account-level Matcher configuration and scoring rules
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the Matcher configuration for the marketplace account, including scoring rules and matcher details used to evaluate received SKUs. The Matcher compares seller-provided SKU data against existing marketplace catalog items to determine approval scores. Returns matcher score thresholds, matcher rules, and configuration details. Returns `404 Not Found` if no matcher is configured for the account. For comprehensive approval settings including specification mappings, use Get Account Approval Settings endpoint instead.
data.reasoning: Original description is unclear about what 'matcher's current approval settings' means and conflates matcher configuration with approval settings. Relationship to Get Account Approval Settings is confusing. Added clarity about Matcher's purpose (comparing SKU data), what is returned, and when to use this vs. the related endpoint. Improved AI agent understanding of the distinction between matcher configuration and broader approval settings.

data.current_description:
Marketplaces use this endpoint to create or update approval settings to a specific seller, on the Received SKUs module. 

The request includes all the details necessary to implement the chosen approval settings.
data.description_suggestion: Creates or updates the approval settings for a specific seller in the Received SKUs module. The request body specifies the seller's MatchFlux approval workflow (default, manual, or autoApprove) and specification mappings for SKU and product fields. Returns the updated seller-specific approval configuration. Seller-level settings override account-level defaults for that seller's received SKUs. Changes take effect immediately for new SKU submissions from the specified seller.
